questions.prompt: Which word means the daughter of your brother or sister?
questions.explanation: Your niece is the daughter of your brother or sister; the son would be your nephew. An aunt is your parent's sister, and a cousin is the child of your aunt or uncle. Niece (girl) and nephew (boy) are a gender pair — don't swap them.

questions.prompt: Find the intruder (the word that is NOT a colour).
questions.explanation: Yellow, brown and grey are all colours. A window is an object in a house, so it is the intruder. Note that grey is the British spelling; American English writes it gray.

questions.prompt: Complete the common phrase: "a ___ of bread".
questions.explanation: We cut bread into pieces, so we say a slice of bread (or a piece of bread). A glass and a bottle hold drinks like water, and a cup holds tea or coffee — none of those goes with bread.

questions.prompt: Which word is a synonym of "cook" (a person who makes food)?
questions.explanation: A chef is another word for a cook — a person whose job is to make food. A farmer grows crops, a driver drives, and a nurse helps sick people, so those are different jobs.
The opposite of expensive (costs a lot of money) is cheap (costs little money). Old is the opposite of new, difficult is the opposite of easy, and small is the opposite of big.

questions.prompt: Which word belongs to the field "school objects"?
questions.explanation: A pencil is a school object you write with. Milk is a drink, an engineer is a job, and Monday is a day of the week, so none of those belongs to school objects.
